message:
fix(fp-conformance): make phase2c test lock poison-tolerant (br-frankenpandas-ijpu)

phase2c_artifact_test_lock previously called .expect() on the lock
result, so any single panicking test cascaded a PoisonError into every
downstream test that touched the lock. Because the guarded inner value
is (), recovering the guard via into_inner() on poison is safe: the
lock only serializes artifact-write side effects, not program state.

Switches .expect(...) to a match-on-poison-into_inner pattern.
Reduces cargo test -p fp-conformance --lib from 334 passed / 18 failed
to 338 / 14 — the 4 recovered tests were pure cascade victims; the
remaining 14 are the distinct root-cause panics unmasked by removing
the cascade (to be tackled in follow-up beads).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
